key_insight: The paper adds rotational dynamics to nonspherical bubble models so viscoelastic soft-material simulations can capture shape evolution, stress transmission, and damping more faithfully.
engineering_relevance: This improves prediction of bubble-driven loading in biomedical treatments and high-strain-rate material tests where nonspherical oscillations affect damage, dissipation, and measurement accuracy.
method: Superpose a rotational deformation contribution with a potential-based perturbation field.
Derive linearized forward and inverse coordinate maps to compute velocity, acceleration, and stress.
Use a Kelvin-Voigt viscoelastic solid with Newtonian viscosity and strain-stiffening neo-Hookean elasticity.
evidence_signal: The model matches prior viscous-fluid limits and agrees with experimental stability and time-evolution data for shape modes from ultrasound forcing and laser-induced cavitation.
watch_item: The formulation is linearized and specialized to the chosen constitutive law, so accuracy may degrade for strongly nonlinear, large-deformation, or highly heterogeneous materials.
paper_title: Nonspherical gas bubble dynamics in viscoelastic soft materials
